programs.name: Medi-Cal Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help pay Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments for eligible low-income seniors. In California, administered via Medi-Cal as Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), and Qualifying Individual (QI).[5]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare enrollees with low income/assets (e.g., QMB: 100% FPL, SLMB: 120% FPL, QI: 135% FPL); seniors/disabled.[5]
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full premiums/costs (~$185/month Part B); SLMB: Part B premium; QI: Part B premium (limited funding).[5]

programs.name: Multipurpose Senior Services Program (MSSP) Waiver
programs.what_it_does: The MSSP Waiver provides home and community-based services (HCBS) as an alternative to nursing facility placement for eligible Medi-Cal seniors. Services include home modifications, personal care, homemaker services, and assistance transitioning from nursing homes.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Medi-Cal eligible individuals aged 60+ who require nursing facility level of care but can safely remain at home; available in counties with MSSP sites.[3]
programs.benefit_value: HCBS up to institutional limits (income $1,836/month individual, assets $130,000); prevents nursing home placement.[1][3]

programs.name: PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: PACE provides comprehensive medical and social services to eligible seniors in community settings, including adult day health care. It is administered through contracted providers in California for frail seniors needing nursing home level care.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Medi-Cal/Medicare eligible seniors 55+ certified for nursing facility level of care, living in PACE service areas.[7]
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive capitation coverage for primary, acute, LTC, and social services; average annual cost ~$60,000 per participant covered by Medicare/Medi-Cal.

programs.name: CalFresh
programs.what_it_does: CalFresh is California's SNAP program providing monthly food benefits via EBT card to buy groceries. It supplements food budgets for low-income seniors to ensure nutritional needs are met.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ (higher asset disregard), households with income ≤130% FPL (~$1,634 individual 2026).[2]
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per senior household; max $291 individual (2026 adjusted).

programs.name: LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP provides grants to help with heating/cooling bills and crisis assistance for energy emergencies. Administered statewide through local agencies for eligible low-income households.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors (≤150% FPL, ~$22,590 individual); priority for 60+.[7]
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants $200-1,000+ for heating/cooling; crisis aid up to $1,800.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce energy costs. Administered by California Department of Community Services and Development for low-income homes.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households ≤200% FPL (~$30,120 individual); priority seniors/disabled.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements averaging $6,000-8,000 per home; lifetime energy savings ~$4,000+.

programs.name: Health Insurance Counseling & Advocacy Program (HICAP)
programs.what_it_does: HICAP (California's SHIP) offers free one-on-one Medicare counseling, plan comparisons, and appeal assistance. Provided through Area Agencies on Aging statewide.[7]
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries 65+, younger disabled, family caregivers in California.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Free counseling; helps save average $500+/year on premiums/coverage choices.

programs.name: Home Delivered Meals (via AAA/ Nutrition Services)
programs.what_it_does: Provides nutritious meals delivered to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Funded through Older Americans Act via California Department of Aging and local AAAs.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, meeting state eligibility.[7]
programs.benefit_value: 5-7 meals/week suggested donation $3-10; ~$8/meal actual cost covered.

programs.name: Family Caregiver Support Program (FCSP)
programs.what_it_does: FCSP provides respite care, counseling, training, and supplies to family caregivers of seniors. Includes short-term relief care and supplemental services through AAAs.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of adults 60+ or 18-59 with dementia/Alzheimer's.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $1,650/year; counseling/training at no/low cost.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: SCSEP provides part-time community service work and training leading to unsubsidized employment. Administered by AAAs and nonprofits for low-income older workers.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income individuals 55+ (≤125% FPL), priority disadvantaged.[7]
programs.benefit_value: 20 hrs/week at minimum wage (~$7,200/year); job placement support.

programs.name: Legal Services for Seniors (via AAAs/Legal Aid)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal assistance for issues like housing, benefits, abuse, and consumer problems. Delivered through Area Agencies on Aging and legal aid organizations.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ unable to afford private legal help.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Free consultations/representation; resolves benefits/housing cases worth thousands.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Ombudsman advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care. Operated through local AAAs statewide.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, RCFEs, and adult care facilities; families.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy; resolves ~85% complaints without litigation, protects rights.
